text: Green hydrogen, which is free of green gas emissions, is a pivotal element of the transition envisioned by the Paris Agreement. While the share of the proton exchange membrane water electrolyzers (PEMWEs) in the low-carbon hydrogen production market remains minor—below a gigawatt, or about one megawatt per day—climate change economics demands scaling up to hundreds of gigawatts by 2030. In this paper, based on feedback from three manufacturers of hydrogen technologies, we provide cell-level technological roadmap to help identify the keys growth factors: cost and economy of scale; factors related to the cost of renewable energy; legislation. This requires performing accelerated stress tests and technical on-material innovations for each component.
explanation: Domain-specific academic tone with plausible content, but some phrasing and a questionable unit (one megawatt per day) suggest possible AI authorship.
